steps.welcome: Welcome to RDF Editor! Let's take a quick tour of the main features.
steps.toolbar: The toolbar contains global actions like saving, loading, and clearing data.
steps.modeToggle: Switch between different RDF domains (like BIM or Schema.org) to get specialized templates and styling.
steps.viewToggle: Switch between Editor, Graph, and Table views, or use the Split view to see both.
steps.templates: Quickly start with predefined RDF templates for your active domain.
steps.editor: Write and edit your RDF data directly in Turtle syntax with autocompletion and syntax highlighting.
steps.graph: Visualize your RDF as an interactive graph. You can add nodes and edges directly from the toolbar.
steps.table: View and edit your triples in a structured table format. Double-click any cell to edit it.
steps.help: Need a refresher? Click this help button to restart this tutorial anytime.

validation.title: SHACL Validation

# Paste your SHACL shapes here
validation.noShapes: Paste SHACL shapes on the left and click Run.
